title: Staffing Firm Placement Capacity Score
subtitle: Find the bottleneck between your team size and your placement volume

persona_selector.text: What best describes your staffing firm right now?

answers.label: Boutique shop, 10-20 employees, founder still in the deals
Growing firm, 21-40 employees, layered ops starting to form
Established firm, 41-75 employees, multiple desks and verticals
Multi-office or multi-vertical, scaling past 50 with ops leadership in place

questions.text: When a new job order comes in from a client, how quickly does a recruiter have a fully workable brief?

answers.label: More than 48 hours, often through back-and-forth emails
24 to 48 hours, depending on who picks it up
Same day, but quality of the brief is inconsistent
Within 4 hours with a standard intake template
Under 1 hour, automated intake form feeds the ATS directly

questions.text: How is a new client job order captured today?

answers.label: Phone call or email, then someone retypes it somewhere
Shared inbox, manually triaged by an account manager
Web form that emails the team, no system entry
Intake form that creates an ATS record automatically
Structured intake form with required fields, ATS record, and Slack or Teams notification to the assigned recruiter

questions.text: How well do you qualify a job order before recruiters start sourcing?

answers.label: We start sourcing the moment the client says go
Account manager asks a few questions, no checklist
We have a qualification checklist, used about half the time
Every job order passes a qualification checklist before sourcing starts
Qualification scorecard with go or no-go criteria, fee, exclusivity, and realistic timeline locked in writing

questions.text: How clean and centralized is your client and contact data?

answers.label: Spreadsheets and inboxes, no single source of truth
CRM exists but data is stale and incomplete
CRM is the source of truth for active accounts only
CRM is current, with required fields and weekly hygiene checks
CRM is fully synced with ATS, email, and calendar, automated enrichment in place

low.headline: Your intake is leaking deals before they even start
low.text: Job orders are entering your firm through too many doors and arriving at recruiters half formed. That kills speed-to-submit, which is the single biggest factor in winning placements. You are losing fees to faster competitors before your team gets a real shot.
steps: Build a single structured intake form, web based, with required fields like role, fee, exclusivity, and timeline
Connect that form to your ATS so a job order record is created automatically
Define a 1 hour SLA from intake submitted to recruiter assigned, and track it weekly

mid.headline: Intake works, but inconsistency is costing you fees
mid.text: You have the bones of a good intake process, but it depends on which person touches the order. Standardization is the next unlock. Tighten the process and your speed-to-submit will jump without adding headcount.
steps: Mandate the qualification checklist on every job order, no exceptions
Add automated CRM and ATS sync so reps stop dual-entering data
Set a weekly intake quality review with your top account manager

high.headline: Intake is a competitive advantage, push it further
high.text: You have a fast, clean, structured intake. Now turn it into a growth lever. Use the data you are already capturing to score account quality and prioritize the orders most likely to close at full fee.
steps: Add an automated job order scoring model based on fee size, exclusivity, and historical fill rate
Auto-route high-score orders to your strongest desk within minutes
Publish a monthly intake performance report tied to placement revenue

questions.text: When a new job order hits a recruiter, how often do they already have qualified candidates ready?

answers.label: Almost never, sourcing starts from scratch every time
Occasionally, for a few repeat roles
About half the time for our most common roles
Most of the time, we maintain talent pools by role family
Always for core roles, with pre-vetted bench candidates ready to submit

questions.text: How is your candidate database maintained?

answers.label: It is a graveyard, most records are over a year stale
Recruiters update records when they remember
Quarterly cleanup, but data quality is mixed
Required fields enforced, monthly hygiene routines
Automated enrichment and re-engagement campaigns keep records current

questions.text: How do you re-engage candidates you have placed or interviewed before?

answers.label: We do not, we go cold after one cycle
Ad hoc outreach by individual recruiters
Quarterly newsletter or check-in
Automated nurture sequences segmented by role and seniority
Multi-channel nurture across email, SMS, and LinkedIn with response tracking

questions.text: How well do you measure pipeline health by role family?

answers.label: We do not measure it, we react to open orders
Recruiters know their own pipeline, no central view
Weekly pipeline meeting using ATS reports
Live dashboard with candidate counts per stage per role family
Predictive view of pipeline coverage versus expected demand by role

low.headline: You are a reactive sourcing shop and it is capping your placements
low.text: Every new job order forces your team to start from zero. That is the most expensive way to recruit and it stretches time-to-fill past the point clients will wait. Building even a small bench will compound revenue fast.
steps: Identify your top 5 most placed role families and build a target list of 50 vetted candidates per family
Stand up automated re-engagement sequences for past placements and silver medalists
Add a weekly pipeline review focused on coverage, not activity

mid.headline: Your pipeline works, but it is not predictive
mid.text: You have pools and some hygiene, but you are still firefighting on harder roles. The shift from reactive to predictive sourcing is where margin lives. You will close faster and protect fees when supply is already there.
steps: Build a live pipeline dashboard showing candidates per stage per role family
Automate weekly nurture touches for inactive but qualified candidates
Forecast hiring demand 60 days out by client and pre-source against it

high.headline: Your pipeline is strong, monetize it
high.text: You have built a real talent asset. Treat it like one. Your bench can support faster submits, retainer pitches, and even productized hiring offers that competitors cannot match.
steps: Package your bench depth into a retainer or exclusive pitch for top accounts
Add candidate scoring to surface the top 10 percent per role family automatically
Open a referral loop inside your placed candidate community

questions.text: How long does it take from receiving a job order to first qualified submission?

answers.label: More than 5 business days
3 to 5 business days
2 business days on average
Same day to next day
Under 4 hours for priority orders

questions.text: How do recruiters qualify a candidate against a job order?

answers.label: Gut feel after a quick resume scan
Phone screen, no standard questions
Phone screen with a checklist used inconsistently
Structured screen with scorecard tied to the job brief
AI-assisted shortlist plus structured human screen with scorecard

questions.text: How much time per day do recruiters spend on repetitive qualification tasks?

answers.label: More than 5 hours, it eats their day
3 to 5 hours
1 to 3 hours
Under 1 hour, most prep is automated
Near zero, AI summaries and pre-screens handle the prep

questions.text: How do you ensure submission quality before sending candidates to clients?

answers.label: Recruiter sends directly, no review
Manager reviews on request
Required peer or manager review for new recruiters only
Standard submission template with required QA fields
Automated QA checks plus manager sign off on every submission

low.headline: Your matching speed is killing your win rate
low.text: Clients hire who shows up first with a strong slate. If your team is taking days to submit, you are losing to faster firms even when your candidates are better. Speed and structure here will move revenue inside 30 days.
steps: Set a hard 24 hour SLA from job order to first qualified submission and track it daily
Build a one-page screening scorecard tied to each job brief and require it on every screen
Add an AI assist for resume summaries and prep notes to free up 5+ hours per recruiter per week

mid.headline: You are quick, now get sharper
mid.text: Your team can submit fast, but qualification consistency is uneven. The next gain is in submission quality, which protects your fee and reputation with clients. Tighter QA also reduces re-work that quietly burns capacity.
steps: Roll out a single submission template with required QA fields across all desks
Run a weekly review of submissions versus client feedback to spot patterns
Add AI screening summaries so recruiters spend time on judgment, not data entry

high.headline: You match fast and clean, raise your ceiling
high.text: Your matching engine is mature. Use the saved time to expand capacity per recruiter or push into higher-fee searches that demand premium quality. This is where you can scale revenue without scaling headcount.
steps: Set per-recruiter capacity targets and measure against them monthly
Move top desks toward retained or exclusive search where your QA wins deals
Productize your screening method as a client-facing differentiator

questions.text: How proactive is communication with active clients during a search?

answers.label: We respond when they ask, rarely reach out first
Updates when there is news, no schedule
Weekly update emails to most accounts
Scheduled cadence with structured updates per role
Automated status updates plus weekly strategic check-in by account manager

questions.text: How well do you track client feedback on submissions?

answers.label: Feedback lives in inboxes and recruiters' heads
Captured in ATS sometimes, not consistent
Required field in ATS for every submission
Captured in ATS and reviewed weekly with the recruiter
Captured, reviewed, and trended by client and role to inform sourcing

questions.text: How do you handle account expansion within existing clients?

answers.label: We wait for the client to send us another order
Account manager checks in occasionally
Quarterly business reviews with top accounts
Defined account growth plan per top 20 client
Account growth plan plus automated trigger when a new role appears at a client

questions.text: How quickly do you respond to client messages during a live search?

answers.label: Sometimes 24 hours or more
Within a business day
Within 4 hours during business hours
Within 1 hour during business hours
Under 30 minutes with shared inbox routing and on-call coverage

low.headline: Your clients feel forgotten and your retention shows it
low.text: Without proactive communication, clients assume you are not working their search. They go quiet, then they go elsewhere. Fixing the cadence is the cheapest, fastest way to protect revenue you already earned.
steps: Set a mandatory weekly update cadence per active search, even when there is no news
Capture submission feedback as a required ATS field and review it weekly
Identify your top 20 accounts and assign a named account owner for each

mid.headline: You communicate well, now make it strategic
mid.text: Your basics are in place but account expansion is mostly accidental. Adding intentional growth plans turns satisfied clients into repeat revenue. This is the highest leverage move for firms your size.
steps: Build a one-page account growth plan for each top 20 client and review quarterly
Set up alerts when a tracked client posts a new role on LinkedIn or their site
Roll out a feedback tracker that trends client responses by role and recruiter

high.headline: Your account engine is strong, scale it
high.text: You are already running a tight client motion. The next leap is to systematize it across more accounts and protect partner-level relationships from key person risk. Documented playbooks make this durable as you grow.
steps: Document your account playbook and onboard every account manager against it
Tier accounts and assign service levels and cadence per tier
Add an executive sponsor program for your top 10 revenue accounts

questions.text: How automated is your placement-to-invoice workflow?

answers.label: Manual handoffs, lots of email and spreadsheet work
Some templates, mostly manual
ATS triggers an invoice request to finance
Automated handoff from ATS to billing system with checks
End to end automated, from placement record to invoice sent and tracked

questions.text: How well are your tools integrated, ATS, CRM, email, calendar, billing?

answers.label: They are all separate, lots of dual entry
A couple of integrations, mostly manual
Core systems integrated, edges still manual
Most systems sync automatically with monitoring
Fully integrated stack with workflow automation across all tools

questions.text: How visible are your operational metrics in real time?

answers.label: Reports built monthly by hand
Weekly reports, manually compiled
Standard dashboard updated weekly
Live dashboards for key metrics by desk
Live dashboards plus alerts when key metrics fall outside thresholds

questions.text: How much repetitive admin work falls on recruiters or account managers?

answers.label: Heavy, more than 2 hours a day
Moderate, 1 to 2 hours a day
Some, around 30 to 60 minutes a day
Minimal, under 30 minutes a day
Near zero, automation and ops support handle it

low.headline: Your back office is the hidden cap on placements
low.text: Manual handoffs, dual entry, and slow reporting are quietly stealing recruiter hours and delaying revenue. You are paying senior people to do junior work. Fixing this frees real capacity without hiring.
steps: Map every handoff between ATS, CRM, email, and billing and target the worst 3 for automation
Move to a single ATS as the source of truth and integrate it with your billing system
Shift admin tasks off recruiters by adding a part-time ops coordinator or virtual assistant

mid.headline: Your stack is decent, the integrations are not
mid.text: You have the tools but they do not talk to each other well enough. The result is silent leakage in time, data quality, and reporting accuracy. Tightening integrations pays back fast in recruiter capacity.
steps: Audit your tool stack and consolidate overlapping systems
Automate the placement to invoice workflow end to end
Stand up live dashboards with thresholds and alerts for key metrics

high.headline: Your operations are mature, turn them into a moat
high.text: Few firms your size run this clean. Lean into it. Your data quality and visibility allow forecasting, capacity planning, and AI assistance that compounds your edge. Aim for revenue per recruiter as your north star metric.
steps: Set a target for revenue per recruiter and align ops investment to move it
Layer AI assistants on top of your clean data for sourcing, screening, and reporting
Build a quarterly capacity model that ties hiring decisions to forecast demand

cta.headline: Want help fixing your weakest category in the next 30 days?
cta.description: Book a 30 minute strategy call with Ivan. We will review your scores, pinpoint the highest revenue lever, and map a concrete automation and operations plan tailored to your firm. No pitch deck, just the playbook.
